items.headline: Spurs bang celebration drum after first playoff series win in nine years
items.blurb: Spurs bang celebration drum after first playoff series win in nine years — Watch as the San Antonio Spurs commemorate toppling the Portland Trail Blazers in their first playoff series win since 2017, breaking out their victory drum as they celebrate with their fans.

items.headline: Spurs cruise past Trail Blazers in Game 5 to clinch series win
items.blurb: Spurs cruise past Trail Blazers in Game 5 to clinch series win — Despite a couple of scares earlier in the matchup, the Spurs cruised to the second round of the NBA Playoffs after beating the Portland Trail Blazers 114-95 in Game 5 on Tuesday night.

items.blurb: Jalen Brunson's epic performance leads Knicks to series-leading win — Facing all of the pressure, the New York Knicks put together their best performance of the series on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Knicks seize control of series with Game 5 domination vs. Hawks
items.blurb: Knicks seize control of series with Game 5 domination vs. Hawks — For the second straight game, they outshot the Hawks from three-point range while dominating inside and are one win away from advancing to the second round.

items.headline: Jalen Brunson scores 39 points as Knicks rout Hawks for 3-2 series lead
items.blurb: Jalen Brunson scores 39 points as Knicks rout Hawks for 3-2 series lead — Jalen Brunson scored 39 points, nearly extending his own franchise record for 40-point playoff games, and the New York Knicks routed the Atlanta Hawks 126-97 on Tuesday night for a 3-2 lead in the first-round playoff series.

items.headline: Luka Doncic Unlikely To Play In Second Round If Lakers Advance
items.blurb: Luka Doncic Unlikely To Play In Second Round If Lakers Advance — Luka Doncic is not expected to be available for the Los Angeles Lakers' second round series against the Oklahoma City Thunder should they advance by defeating the Houston Rockets. Doncic has been out since April 2nd with a hamstring injury. "My understanding is it is a slow path on the recovery path," said Shams Charania on ESPN on T

items.blurb: Hawks’ Kuminga takes flight to throw down nasty one-handed lob on Knicks — Watch as Gabe Vincent throws a high lob to Jonathan Kuminga, who takes flight to throw down the nasty one-handed slam.

items.headline: Embiid knocks down deadly fadeaway to help Sixers pull away from Celtics
items.blurb: Embiid knocks down deadly fadeaway to help Sixers pull away from Celtics — Watch as Joel Embiid goes to work on Neemias Queta in the post and knocks down the deadly fadeaway to help the Sixers take their largest lead of the game against the Celtics.

items.headline: NCAA Tournament Expansion To 76 Teams On Track For 2026-27
items.blurb: NCAA Tournament Expansion To 76 Teams On Track For 2026-27 — The NCAA has launched the final steps toward expanding both the men's and women's basketball tournaments from 68 to 76 teams, sources told ESPN. The move is expected to be formalized within weeks and would take effect for the upcoming 2026-27 season, with a mid-May announcement possible. Remaining committee approvals are considered proce

items.headline: Embiid shines as 76ers down Celtics in Game 5 to stave off elimination
items.blurb: Embiid shines as 76ers down Celtics in Game 5 to stave off elimination — Joel Embiid scored 33 points, Tyrese Maxey had 25 points and 10 rebounds, and the Philadelphia 76ers avoided elimination, beating the Boston Celtics 113-97 in Game 5 of their first-round playoff series on Tuesday night.

items.headline: Joel Embiid keeps 76ers alive with massive Game 5 effort
items.blurb: Joel Embiid keeps 76ers alive with massive Game 5 effort — A limited Embiid was good enough to stave off elimination for the Philadelphia 76ers in their 113-97 win over the Boston Celtics.

items.headline: Consensus No. 1 2027 NBA Draft Prospect Tyran Stokes Commits To Kansas Over Kentucky
items.blurb: Consensus No. 1 2027 NBA Draft Prospect Tyran Stokes Commits To Kansas Over Kentucky — Tyran Stokes, the nation's top-ranked senior and consensus favorite to be the No. 1 pick in the 2027 NBA Draft, has committed to Kansas, he announced Tuesday. The 6-foot-7 small forward from Rainier Beach High School in Seattle chose the Jayhawks over Kentucky following an intense months-long recruiting battle.

items.headline: Three things Magic are doing right vs. Pistons
items.blurb: Three things Magic are doing right vs. Pistons — What's the best word to describe a team that survives the play-in tournament, wins the first game of a series against the Eastern Conference top seed and seizes a commanding 3-1 lead? The word you're looking for is Magic.

items.blurb: NBA Presents '3-2-1 Lottery' Anti-Tanking Reform To GMs; Owners Vote May 28 — The NBA has presented a sweeping draft overhaul to its 30 general managers called the "3-2-1 lottery," a reform that would expand the draft lottery from 14 to 16 teams and introduce a relegation zone penalizing the league's three worst records, sources told ESPN. items.blurb: Steve Kerr's Future With Warriors Could Come Down To Negotiating More Than Sentimentality — Steve Kerr and the Golden State Warriors are expected to soon meet to discuss whether he will continue his tenure as head coach. items.headline: Kevin Durant Not Expected To Play In Game 5 Due To Ankle Injury
items.blurb: Kevin Durant Not Expected To Play In Game 5 Due To Ankle Injury — Kevin Durant is expected to miss Game 5 of the Houston Rockets' first round series at the Los Angeles Lakers. Durant has missed the past two games due to a sprained left ankle and bone bruise. Durant also missed Game 1 of the series due to a knee injury he picked up during practice leading to the series. items.headline: Franz Wagner Diagnosed With Right Calf Strain, Status For Game 5 Uncertain
items.blurb: Franz Wagner Diagnosed With Right Calf Strain, Status For Game 5 Uncertain — Franz Wagner has been diagnosed with a right calf strain and his status for Game 5 in uncertain. The Orlando Magic hold a 3-1 series lead over the Detroit Pistons following their Game 4 win. Wagner exited Game 4 with the injury and underwent an MRI on Tuesday. items.blurb: ‘Play our style of basketball’: Barnes on how Raptors can secure Game 5 — Toronto Raptors star forward Scottie Barnes meets with the media and discusses what the team must do in Wednesday’s Game 5 in Cleveland against the Cavaliers in order to take a 3-2 series lead.

items.headline: ‘Doing whatever it takes’: Rajakovic on how Barrett is helping Raptors
items.blurb: ‘Doing whatever it takes’: Rajakovic on how Barrett is helping Raptors — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic meets with the media and discusses the impact that forward RJ Barrett has had on the team in the first round against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: Shining in first playoff experience, Darko Rajakovic never doubted himself, Raptors
items.blurb: Shining in first playoff experience, Darko Rajakovic never doubted himself, Raptors — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic has had a stellar start to his first playoff experience as an NBA head coach, which shouldn’t be surprising given the foundation he laid when he first took the job, writes Michael Grange.

items.headline: Brad Stevens Wins NBA Executive Of The Year For Second Time
items.blurb: Brad Stevens Wins NBA Executive Of The Year For Second Time — Boston Celtics President of Basketball Operations Brad Stevens has been named the 2025-26 NBA Basketball Executive of the Year. Stevens claimed the award for the second time, having previously won it in 2023-24. Stevens earned 69 total points in voting, collecting 11 first-place votes, four second-place votes and two third-place votes.

items.blurb: Darryn Peterson's Work Ethic Not In Question As NBA Teams Focus On Health — Front office sources across the NBA have offered largely positive assessments of Kansas guard Darryn Peterson, describing him as a dedicated worker passionate about his craft. items.blurb: Nuggets Force Game 6 Behind Jokic's Triple-Double; McDaniels: 'We Going To Win The Next One' — Nikola Jokic delivered a 27-point, 16-assist, 12-rebound triple-double Monday to keep the Denver Nuggets' season alive, as they defeated the Minnesota Timberwolves 125-113 in Game 5. items.blurb: Devin Booker Says Thunder Have 'All The Answers To The Test' Following Sweep — The Oklahoma City Thunder eliminated the Phoenix Suns in a first-round sweep Monday, winning Game 4 by a 131-122 final. Oklahoma City has now won 12 consecutive first-round games , sweeping the opening round in each of the past three postseasons. items.headline: Victor Wembanyama: Double-doubles in victory
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama: Double-doubles in victory — Wembanyama generated 17 points (5-7 FG, 1-2 3Pt, 6-6 FT), 14 rebounds, six blocks and three assists across 34 minutes in Tuesday's 114-95 win over the Trail Blazers in Game 5 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Austin Reaves Optimistic About Returning For Game 5
items.blurb: Austin Reaves Optimistic About Returning For Game 5 — Los Angeles Lakers guard Austin Reaves is optimistic about returning for Game 5 of his team's first-round playoff series against the Houston Rockets. The Lakers lead 3-1 and have a chance to close out the series at home in Los Angeles on Wednesday night. Reaves was reportedly close to playing in Game 4, but with a 3-0 series lead, the Lakers ga

items.headline: Jalen Johnson: Stuffs stat sheet in defeat
items.blurb: Jalen Johnson: Stuffs stat sheet in defeat — Johnson finished Tuesday's 126-97 loss to the Knicks in Game 5 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs with 18 points (7-15 FG, 1-5 3Pt, 3-3 FT), 10 rebounds, six assists and two steals across 35 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.blurb: Jalen Brunson: Erupts for 39 points in win — Brunson contributed 39 points (15-23 FG, 3-5 3Pt, 6-8 FT), eight assists and three rebounds across 35 minutes in Tuesday's 126-97 win over the Hawks in Game 5 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jayson Tatum: Double-doubles in defeat
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum: Double-doubles in defeat — Tatum tallied 24 points (8-19 FG, 4-12 3Pt, 4-7 FT), 16 rebounds, four assists and three steals in 41 minutes during Tuesday's 113-97 loss to the 76ers in Game 5 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Joel Embiid: Paces Philadelphia in win
items.blurb: Joel Embiid: Paces Philadelphia in win — Embiid recorded 33 points (12-23 FG, 0-5 3Pt, 9-10 FT), eight assists, four rebounds and one block across 39 minutes in Tuesday's 113-97 win over the Celtics in Game 5 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Dylan Mingo Commits To Baylor
items.blurb: Dylan Mingo Commits To Baylor — Five-star guard Dylan Mingo committed to the Baylor Bears on Monday for the 2026 class. Mingo previously committed to North Carolina, but decommitted after the school fired Hubert Davis. "I believe coach [Scott] Drew was a big factor in helping VJ Edgecombe improve and prepare for the NBA," Mingo said. "He had some tough moments as a freshman and coach Drew helped h

items.headline: Franz Wagner To Undergo MRI On Sore Right Calf
items.blurb: Franz Wagner To Undergo MRI On Sore Right Calf — Orlando Magic forward Franz Wagner will undergo an MRI on his sore right calf on Tuesday. Wagner left the Magic's Game 4 win over the Pistons on Monday in the third quarter with the injury. Wagner added that he hopes to play in Game 5. With the Magic up 3-1 in the series, Game 5 will take place on Wednesday at Detroit.

items.headline: Jamahl Mosley Following Magic Win To Take 3-1 Series Lead: 'Right Now It Means Nothing'
items.blurb: Jamahl Mosley Following Magic Win To Take 3-1 Series Lead: 'Right Now It Means Nothing' — The Orlando Magic defeated the Detroit Pistons 94-88 in Game 4 on Monday night to take a 3-1 series lead. "We put our ourselves in position to try to get four, but right now it means nothing," Magic coach Jamahl Mosley said. "We have the advantage and now we have to try and make sure we keep that advantage."
